rwanda.name: Rwanda Safaris
rwanda.meta: East Africa · Gorillas, rainforest & Lake Kivu
rwanda.card_desc: Volcanoes gorilla trekking, Akagera Big Five, Nyungwe rainforest and lakeside towns on Lake Kivu.
paragraphs: Rwanda - the Land of a Thousand Hills - packs extraordinary biodiversity and culture into one of Africa's most compact and welcoming nations. From misty volcanoes to savannah plains and ancient rainforests, every landscape tells a story of conservation and renewal.

Volcanoes National Park made Rwanda synonymous with mountain gorilla tracking, while Akagera's restored lion and rhino populations bring the Big Five within reach. Nyungwe Forest shelters chimpanzees and a world-class canopy walkway.
Charming towns along Lake Kivu and the immaculate capital Kigali add urban sophistication to any itinerary - making Rwanda the perfect complement to a Tanzania or Uganda safari.
highlights: Mountain Gorilla Tracking - Volcanoes National Park in the Virunga Mountains
Big Five Safari - Akagera National Park savannah and wetlands
Nyungwe Forest - chimpanzees, canopy walkway and 300+ bird species
Lake Kivu - lakeside relaxation in Rubavu and Karongi
Kigali - clean, safe capital with museums and vibrant dining

packages.name: 3 Days Rwanda Gorilla Trek
packages.description: Essential Volcanoes National Park gorilla trek based in Musanze with dramatic Virunga volcano views.

packages.name: 5 Days Rwanda Gorillas & Lake Kivu
packages.description: Gorilla trekking in the Virungas followed by kayaking and lakeside relaxation on Lake Kivu.

packages.name: 6 Days Rwanda Wildlife & Forest Safari
packages.description: Big Five game drives in Akagera and chimpanzee tracking with canopy walkway in Nyungwe Forest.

destinations.name: Volcanoes National Park
destinations.card_desc: Track mountain gorillas in the rainforest of the Virunga volcanoes.
paragraphs: In the heart of Central Africa, great old volcanoes tower almost 15,000 feet, nearly covered with rich green rainforest - the Virungas. Volcanoes National Park is where Dian Fossey pioneered gorilla conservation.
Guided treks bring visitors face to face with habituated mountain gorilla families amid bamboo and hagenia forest on the slopes of Bisoke, Karisimbi and other dormant peaks.
Golden monkey tracking, visits to the Dian Fossey Gorilla Fund and hikes to crater lakes reward travellers who extend their stay beyond the iconic gorilla encounter.

destinations.name: Akagera National Park
destinations.card_desc: Savannah, woodland and lakes where lions and rhinos roam again.
paragraphs: The relatively warm and low-lying plains of Akagera comprise savannah, woodland, wetland and a dozen lakes along Rwanda's border with Tanzania.
In partnership with African Parks, lions and rhinos have been reintroduced - meaning visitors can once again hope to see the Big Five on game drives through diverse habitats.
Boat trips on Lake Ihema reveal hippo, crocodile and waterbirds, while night drives and behind-the-scenes conservation tours add depth to Rwanda's premier savannah experience.

destinations.name: Nyungwe National Park
destinations.card_desc: One of Africa's oldest rainforests - chimps, monkeys and a canopy walkway.
paragraphs: Nyungwe is one of the oldest rainforests in Africa, rich in biodiversity and spectacularly beautiful. The mountainous region teams with wildlife, including chimpanzees and 12 other primate species.
The canopy walkway suspended above the forest floor is among East Africa's most thrilling experiences, while waterfall trails and tea plantation visits frame the park's emerald ridges.
Nyungwe connects naturally with Lake Kivu towns and Rwanda's southern cultural circuit, offering a lush counterpoint to the volcanoes and savannah.

destinations.name: Gishwati Mukura National Park
destinations.card_desc: Rwanda's newest park - chimpanzees and golden monkeys in restored forest.
paragraphs: Gishwati Mukura is Rwanda's fourth national park, made up of two separate forests - the larger Gishwati and smaller Mukura - in the country's western highlands.
Reforestation efforts have created habitat for chimpanzees, golden monkeys, blue and L'Hoest's monkeys, plus a host of birds and smaller forest animals.
Community-based tourism initiatives let visitors experience conservation success firsthand on guided forest walks and cultural exchanges with neighbouring villages.

destinations.name: Kigali
destinations.card_desc: Rwanda's immaculate capital - museums, markets and world-class dining.
paragraphs: Kigali is one of Africa's cleanest and safest capitals, spread across verdant hills with a cosmopolitan atmosphere that belies Rwanda's compact size.
The Kigali Genocide Memorial provides essential context for understanding modern Rwanda, while craft markets, art galleries and the vibrant Nyamirambo neighbourhood showcase contemporary culture.
Excellent hotels and restaurants make Kigali the natural start or end point for gorilla trekking, Akagera safaris and Lake Kivu escapes.

destinations.name: Nyanza
destinations.card_desc: Home to the King's Palace - a beautifully crafted traditional royal residence.
paragraphs: Nyanza was the historical seat of the Rwandan monarchy. The King's Palace is a reconstruction of the traditional royal residence - a beautifully crafted thatched dwelling shaped like a beehive.
The adjacent museum displays regalia and artefacts of the Rwandan kings, while long-horned Inyambo cattle graze on the palace grounds as living symbols of royal tradition.
Nyanza sits on the road between Kigali and Huye, making it an easy cultural stop on Rwanda's southern circuit.

destinations.name: Huye
destinations.card_desc: Southern Rwanda's intellectual and cultural centre, gateway to Nyungwe Forest.
paragraphs: Huye - formerly Butare - is southern Rwanda's main town and intellectual centre, home to the National University and the Ethnographic Museum, one of East Africa's finest collections of Rwandan heritage.
The town's orderly streets, cafes and colonial-era buildings offer a relaxed base before heading west to Nyungwe National Park or south toward Burundi.
Cultural performances, artisan cooperatives and the nearby arboretum make Huye essential for travellers seeking depth beyond gorilla trekking.

destinations.name: Rubavu
destinations.card_desc: Waterfront town on Lake Kivu with sandy beaches and tropical ease.
paragraphs: Rubavu is a waterfront town on the shores of Lake Kivu, with red sandy beaches, warm clean water and an easygoing tropical character.
Once known as Gisenyi, Rubavu faces the Congolese city of Goma across the lake and offers kayaking, boat trips, beach relaxation and sunset dining along the waterfront.
The town is the perfect lakeside finale after gorilla trekking in nearby Volcanoes National Park - just a short drive from Musanze through terraced hills.

destinations.name: Karongi
destinations.card_desc: Palm-fringed Lake Kivu shores and island boat trips from a peaceful resort town.
paragraphs: Karongi - formerly Kibuye - is one of Lake Kivu's most scenic towns, with palm-fringed shores, terraced hills and calm waters ideal for boat trips to Napoleon and other islands.
The town offers a quieter lakeside experience than Rubavu, with excellent lodges perched on peninsulas overlooking the lake and distant Congolese mountains.
Karongi lies midway along Lake Kivu's eastern shore, connecting Rubavu in the north with Rusizi and Nyungwe in the south.

destinations.name: Musanze
destinations.card_desc: Gateway town to Volcanoes National Park and the Virunga foothills.
paragraphs: Musanze - formerly Ruhengeri - is the bustling gateway to Volcanoes National Park and the primary base for gorilla trekking in Rwanda.
The town offers a full range of accommodation, restaurants and tour operators, with dramatic views of the Virunga volcanoes rising above surrounding farmland.
Twin Lakes Burera and Ruhondo, cave explorations and community walks add activities for rest days between gorilla permits.

destinations.name: Rusizi
destinations.card_desc: Border town and practical base for self-drive visitors to Nyungwe National Park.
paragraphs: Rusizi is a small town on the border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo and the closest town to Nyungwe National Park.
Budget and mid-range accommodation here appeals to self-drive visitors planning chimpanzee tracking and canopy walkway adventures in Nyungwe.
The town sits where the Rusizi River flows from Lake Kivu toward Lake Tanganyika, offering a frontier atmosphere at Rwanda's southwestern tip.

destinations.name: Kibeho
destinations.card_desc: Rwanda's sacred pilgrimage site in the hills of the southern province.
paragraphs: Kibeho is a small town in southern Rwanda known as a Marian pilgrimage site, where apparitions reported in the 1980s drew international attention from the Catholic Church.
The Shrine of Our Lady of Kibeho welcomes pilgrims and curious visitors alike, set amid rolling hills and farming communities typical of rural Rwanda.
Kibeho adds a spiritual dimension to the southern circuit between Huye and Nyungwe, particularly during annual pilgrimage events.

destinations.name: Lake Kivu
destinations.card_desc: Africa's sixth-largest lake - beaches, islands and terraced hillsides.
paragraphs: Lake Kivu is one of Africa's Great Lakes, forming Rwanda's western border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Its deep, methane-rich waters and terraced shores create a stunning inland seascape.
Resort towns Rubavu and Karongi offer beaches, boating and fresh tilapia dining, while island-hopping trips reveal fishing communities and birdlife along the shoreline.
A Lake Kivu extension perfectly follows gorilla trekking or Nyungwe forest adventures - relaxation without leaving Rwanda's compact borders.
